propositions: Add an IntrospectionTokenVerifier that POSTs opaque tokens to an RFC 7662 /introspect endpoint, accepts only active:true, and maps the response into an AccessToken stamped with resource=auth_resource_server_url.

Activate the verifier by including 'introspection' in mcp.auth_strategies and register flat config keys (endpoint, client_id, client_secret_env, allow_private_host, allow_missing_aud) in McpConfig and _SECTIONS['mcp'], deriving REBAR_MCP_AUTH_INTROSPECTION_* env-var names into MCP_ENV_VARS and server.json.

Enforce fail-closed security: HTTPS-only endpoint, client_secret_basic auth with secret from env var (required non-empty at startup), bounded HTTP timeouts, and rejection of private/link-local hosts unless allow_private_host=true.
Validate audience per §2.2 accepting string or array forms; reject aud-absent tokens by default unless allow_missing_aud=true, and reject mismatched audiences.
Treat non-200 status, timeouts, and malformed responses (non-JSON body or missing boolean active) as raising errors that deny, never accept.

Map optional RFC 7662 fields with defined fallbacks: split space-delimited scope, set expires_at from exp (None when absent, never 0), subject from sub, client_id falling back to sub then 'unknown'; no caching in v1 so every request re-introspects for instant revocation.
